reasoning: The resolution criteria specify that the protest must have a start date strictly AFTER the launch of this question. Given the question includes a Wikipedia link to the '2025 Turkish protests' which began on March 19, 2025, it is highly likely this question was launched after that date, meaning the current massive anti-government protests (sparked by Ekrem Imamoglu's arrest) will not count toward a YES resolution. The Global Protest Tracker (GPT) methodology groups related protests into continuous movements with a single initial start date. Therefore, any ongoing demonstrations in May or June 2025 will simply be appended to the March 19 tracker entry rather than logged as a new protest. For this question to resolve YES, an entirely distinct anti-government movement with a new trigger and over 10,000 participants would need to start before July 1, 2025. Given the short timeframe (under 2 months remaining) and the fact that current opposition energy is already fully absorbed by the Imamoglu protests, a separate mass movement is highly unlikely to emerge and be tracked independently by the GPT.
